name: Unimodular gravity
summary: A formulation of gravity in which the metric determinant is fixed (sqrt(-g) = 1) and the Einstein equations are reformulated so that the cosmological constant appears as an integration constant of the constrained variational principle rather than as a Lagrangian parameter. QFT vacuum-energy contributions do not source curvature.

addresses_puzzles.note: Removes the radiative-correction component but reintroduces the 'why this value' question as a boundary condition. Does not explain why the integration constant takes the meV^4 value; relabels the puzzle rather than resolving it.

Reformulation with fixed metric determinant; addresses the cosmological-constant problem via decoupling but does not resolve the UV problem of perturbative GR (Anderson-Finkelstein 1971; Smolin 2009).

touches_benchmarks.note: Equivalent to GR at the classical level for trace-free Einstein equations; differs only in the off-shell role of Lambda.

introduces.note: An integration constant takes the role of Lambda; not a fundamental free parameter in the Lagrangian sense.

history: Einstein (1919) formulated unimodular gravity. Discussed by Weinberg (1989) and Henneaux-Teitelboim (1989) as a partial response to the cosmological-constant problem. Quantum versions explored by Smolin and others.

references.citation: Weinberg, 'The cosmological constant problem', RMP 61, 1 (1989), section IV
Henneaux and Teitelboim, 'The cosmological constant and general covariance', Phys. Lett. B 222, 195 (1989)
